title: rhinanthus wagneri subsp. caroli-henrici
intro: Rhinanthus wagneri subsp. caroli-henrici, also known as the Carlsfield broomrape, is a rare, parasitic plant species that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family and is native to the Alpine regions of Central Europe. This plant is known to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, which can be beneficial for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Rhinanthus wagneri subsp. caroli-henrici include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the body's natural defenses against infections. Traditionally, herbal preparations of this plant have been used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use and efficacy are not extensively documented in modern medical literature.
